title: OpenAI Plans to Invest 20 Billion Dollars in the US to Build a Large-Scale Data Center
excerpt: In the endless arms race of artificial intelligence, OpenAI has once again pushed the stakes forward. According to Bloomberg, the company plans to invest hundre
content_html:
In the endless arms race of artificial intelligence, OpenAI has once again pushed the stakes forward. According to Bloomberg, the company plans to invest hundreds of billions of dollars to build a new ultra-large-scale data center near Savannah, Georgia, in the United States.

This massive facility will be located within a data center park in Effingham County. OpenAI has already signed a contract with Georgia Power to secure 3.2 gigawatts of energy supply—this level of power is enough to make it one of OpenAI's largest projects to date. According to the plan, hundreds of megawatts of electricity will be put into use starting in 2028, while the remaining part will continue to be built until 2032. This is not a one-time completion project, but a continuous construction that spans several years.

In order to qualify for a local incentive policy, OpenAI has committed to an investment of 20 billion US dollars (approximately 135.534 billion RMB at the current exchange rate), and this policy will soon go to a voting session. Sachin Katti, Vice President of Computational Strategy at OpenAI, calculated a larger figure: if this data center reaches its full capacity of 3.2 gigawatts, the total cost is likely to exceed 30 billion US dollars. In other words, the 20 billion currently committed is just a part of the final bill for this campus.

The data center in Georgia is just the tip of the iceberg. According to sources cited by the Wall Street Journal, OpenAI has raised its projected computing power expenditure through 2030 to nearly 750 billion US dollars (approximately 5.08 trillion RMB). Just a few months ago, this number was around 600 billion US dollars. Within a few months, the projection has increased by about 150 billion US dollars—this reflects both a re-evaluation of computing power demand and a public gamble.

For competitors also laying down AI infrastructure, such figures mean they have to recalculate their costs. When OpenAI bets on computing power for the next five years at the level of 750 billion US dollars, the entire industry's cost benchmarks may have to shift accordingly.

sentences.zh: 当OpenAI以7500亿美元的水平押注未来五年的算力时，整个行业的成本基准可能不得不相应转移。

content_html_zh:
在无休无止的人工智能军备竞赛中，OpenAI再次将赌注推向了前进。 据彭博社报道，该公司计划投资数千亿美元在美国佐治亚州萨凡纳附近新建一个超大规模数据中心。

这个大型设施将位于埃芬汉县的一个数据中心园区内。 OpenAI 已经与佐治亚电力公司签署了一份合同，以确保 3.2 吉瓦的能源供应——这一水平的电力足以使其成为 OpenAI 迄今为止最大的项目之一。 根据计划，数百兆瓦电力将从2028年开始投入使用，剩余部分将继续建设到2032年。 这不是一次性完成的项目，而是跨越数年的持续建设。

为了获得当地激励政策的资格，OpenAI已承诺投资200亿美元（按当前汇率约合人民币1355.34亿元），该政策即将进入投票阶段。 OpenAI计算战略副总裁Sachin Katti算了一个更大的数字：如果这个数据中心达到3.2吉瓦的满负荷容量，总成本很可能超过300亿美元。 也就是说，目前承诺的200亿只是这个校园最终账单的一部分。

佐治亚州的数据中心只是冰山一角。 据《华尔街日报》援引消息人士透露，OpenAI已将到2030年的预计算力支出上调至近7500亿美元（约合5.08万亿元人民币）。 就在几个月前，这个数字还在6000亿美元左右。 几个月内，这一预测增加了约 1500 亿美元——这既反映了对算力需求的重新评估，也反映了一场公开的赌博。

对于同样铺设人工智能基础设施的竞争对手来说，这些数字意味着他们必须重新计算成本。 当OpenAI以7500亿美元的水平押注未来五年的算力时，整个行业的成本基准可能不得不相应转移。
